title: Cambridge launches major strategic partnership with IonQ to
why:
- The University of Cambridge is establishing the IonQ Quantum Innovation Centre, which will house a state-of-the-art 256-qubit IonQ quantum computer—the most powerful quantum computer in the UK upon installation.
- This collaboration marks the University of Cambridge’s largest-ever corporate research partnership and the first of its kind for a UK university.
- The Centre will support a wide research portfolio in quantum computing, quantum networks, quantum sensing, and quantum security, with participation from academic and commercial partners.
- Innovate UK will provide access and computing time on the new quantum computer for the UK’s National Quantum Computing Centre, enabling researchers and startups across the UK to use the resource.
- The partnership will fund new academic positions, postdoctoral fellows, and PhD students at Cambridge, helping to nurture the next generation of quantum scientists and technologists.
